_comment: Shop-interior dialogue catalog for the shop-life integration (#65). PER-ROLE packs (not per-character) so one voice covers every staffer of that role. Categories per pack are role-specific; see SHOPDIALOG.md for trigger mapping. Voice assignment lives in _voices (resolved by shopvoicegen.js). Emotion markers must START the sentence: (scared)(angry)(shouting)(laughing)(sighing)(happy)(whispering)(effort)(whining) — psxify.speakable() strips a single leading (marker) before TTS. Draft 2026-07-09, keep lines under ~3s.

CASHIER.desc: NEW 'Walmart cashier' — friendly middle-aged female, conversational

BARISTA.desc: NEW 'Friendly Coffee Enthusiast' — young female, upbeat

chatA.note: round-robin DIFFERENT staff role voices (responder), so a Q/A pair is two people

CUSTOMER.note: REUSE existing NPC voices, round-robin (ped_f, ped_old, GLORIA, SKYLER-teen, BRAD, KEISHA). SKYLER lands the kids/whiny lines by index. No new refs.

greet: Hi there! Did you find everything okay today?
Welcome in! Find everything you were looking for?
(happy) Hey there, how you doing today?
Afternoon! You got your Publix card with you?
Hi! Paper or plastic today, hon?
Come on down, register's open right here.
total: Alright, that comes to twenty three fifty.
Your total today is eleven dollars even.
That'll be forty seven and sixty two cents.
Okay, eighty nine dollars and change, whenever you're ready.
Comes to six dollars flat, easy one.
Grand total, one hundred and two dollars, hon.
smalltalk: Ooh, these are on buy one get one this week, good grab.
This weather, right? Can't decide if it wants to rain.
Love this brand, my whole family eats it.
You must be having a cookout, look at all this.
Careful, that watermelon's heavier than it looks.
bag: You want these bagged together or separate, hon?
I'll double-bag the cold stuff for you.
Want me to leave the milk out of the bag?
receipt: Here's your receipt, your savings are right at the bottom.
You saved twelve dollars today, look at that.
nextinline: I can help whoever's next over here!
Register four's open, I got you.
answer: Milk? That's the back wall, aisle one, all the way down.
Bread's on aisle six, hon, near the bakery.
Oh, that one's gluten free, yeah, says so right on the label.
Let me do a price check on that real quick.
Eggs are in the cold case by the deli.
We're all out of those, more comes in Thursday.

grumble: (sighing) Who put the soup with the cereal again?
Man, this pallet's been sitting here since we opened.
Third time I've faced this shelf today, third time.
Whoever ordered forty cases of ranch, we gotta talk.
(sighing) My back is done. Done, I tell you.
effort: (effort) Okay, up we go, big one.
Ugh, these water cases weigh a ton.
(effort) Lift with the legs, lift with the legs.
Heavy box coming through, watch your feet.
aisle: That's on aisle five, halfway down on your left.
Peanut butter? Aisle seven, next to the jelly, obviously.
Cleaning stuff's all the way in the back, aisle twelve.
You want the chips, that's aisle three, my friend.
Frozen's on the far wall, can't miss it.
restock: Fresh stock going up on aisle nine, folks.
Just filled the soda, help yourselves.
Careful, wet floor sign's up, I just mopped.
Restocking the cereal, scuse the boxes.
quip: I just work here, man, I don't make the prices.
You seen how much cereal people buy? It's wild.

call: Now serving number forty two! Forty two!
Who's got ticket fifty one? Fifty one, right here.
Number sixty, your order's up, hon.
Take a ticket, I'll be right with you.
suggest: The turkey is real good today, want it sliced thin?
You want that half pound or a full pound, sweetie?
Try the rotisserie, it just came out the oven.
Potato salad's fresh made this morning, want a scoop?

call: Number's up! Who ordered the ribeye?
Got a fresh cut ready back here!
Come on over, I'll cut it to order for ya.
suggest: These pork chops are beautiful today, real thick.
You want that ground fresh? Two minutes, I'll do it.
The brisket's on special, whole thing for the smoker.
Bone in or bone out on that chicken, chief?

order: I've got a venti pink drink for the counter!
Order up for... is it Aidan? A-A-Ron? I'm so sorry.
Tall blonde roast, room for cream, right here!
Iced brown sugar oat shaken espresso, whew, for Sam!
Double shot on the bar for Jessica!
Whoever ordered the eight pump vanilla, no judgment, it's ready.
Mobile order for Taylor, it's up!
chatter: Welcome in! What can I get started for you?
You want that hot or iced today?
Ooh, the fall drinks just dropped, they're so good.
Name for the cup? Don't worry, I'll still spell it wrong.
Whipped cream on that? I always say yes.
For here or to go, hon?
One sec, gotta grab more oat milk.
hiss: That hissing? Totally normal, it's the espresso.
Sorry, the grinder's loud, what was that?
Pulling your shots now, thirty seconds.
Hold on, blender's going, I can't hear a thing!

order: Medium iced coffee, extra extra, order's ready!
Order up! Half dozen donuts, still warm.
Got a large hot, cream and three sugars!
Sausage egg and cheese on a bagel, coming out!
Two boston kremes and a coolatta, whose is it?
Number's up for the drive-thru, pull around!
chatter: Welcome to Dunkin, what can I get ya?
America runs on this stuff, am I right?
Fresh donuts just came out the fryer.
You want that iced? Everybody wants it iced.
Munchkins are ten for three bucks today.
quip: The frozen machine is down again, of course it is.
I've made forty coffees and it's not even nine.
Yeah, we got pumpkin. We always got pumpkin.
Whole box of Joe? Somebody's got a meeting.

call: Omakase for the gentleman? I take good care of you.
Order up! One dragon roll, hai!
Fresh tuna today, very good, very fresh.
Sit at the bar, I make it right in front of you.
phrase: This one, hamachi, yellowtail, melt in your mouth.
Little wasabi, not too much, I put it for you already.
You like it spicy? I make the spicy tuna, extra kick.
The eel, unagi, my specialty, you must try.
Hai, hai, coming right up, one moment please.
Handmade, every piece, no machine here.

greet: Next in line, please. The window's open.
Welcome to Regions. How can I help you today.
Do you have your account number or your ID.
Step right up, I don't bite. Usually.
transaction: How would you like that, are twenties okay.
That's going to need a manager's signature, one moment.
I'll need to see two forms of ID for that.
Your available balance is on the receipt.
Sign right here, and here, and initial there.
deadpan: (sighing) The pens are chained down for a reason, sir.
No, we can't waive that fee. I've tried. Believe me.
The lollipops are for the kids. Fine, take one.
The vault? That's above my pay grade, funny guy.
Have a nice day. Truly. I mean it. Mostly.

joke: Everything's a dollar twenty five now, don't shoot the messenger.
Yes, that's a dollar. That too. All of it. A dollar.
You'd be amazed what a dollar still buys in here.
We got everything, long as everything costs a dollar.
That name brand? A dollar. How? Don't ask questions.
Party supplies, aisle two, and yes, still a dollar.
Best deal in Westchase, and I'm not even lying.
line: Need a bag? That's a dime now, sorry hon.
We just got the seasonal stuff in, real cute.
Careful, aisle three's a little cluttered.
Balloons? I can blow up balloons, gimme a minute.

pa: Attention shoppers, we need a cleanup on aisle three.
Price check for register two, price check register two.
Would the owner of a blue sedan please move your vehicle.
Attention guests, our deli special ends at six today.
Cashiers to the front, we've got a line building up.
Lost and found, we have a set of keys at guest services.
The store will be closing in fifteen minutes, please bring your items forward.
pep: Great hustle today, team, let's keep it up.
Somebody cover aisle nine, Carl's on break.
Smile, folks, the customers can hear it in your voice.
Who's on carts? We need carts up front.
escalate: Sir, I'm the manager. What seems to be the problem.
Let's take this to my office, shall we.
I'm going to have to ask you to leave the store.
Everybody stay calm, I've got this handled.

chatQ: You catch what time you're on the schedule tomorrow?
Did you hear what that lady said to me at register two?
You take your break yet, or you covering me first?
How short did your drawer come up last night?
You seen the new guy? He put the milk on the wrong shelf again.
Manager say anything about the holiday hours yet?
You smell that? Somebody left something in the break room fridge.
Whose turn is it to do carts in this heat?
You believe they cut our hours again this week?
Did the truck come in yet, or are we still waiting?
You want anything? I'm running to Dunkin on my break.
Guess who called out sick again today.
You closing tonight, or is that me?
They fix the AC back here yet, or nah?
You hear corporate's sending somebody to inspect Friday?
How many returns you get today? I got, like, six.
You seen my box cutter? I swear I just had it.
Break room coffee's empty again, who does that?
Third register just went down, you got a screwdriver?
You clock out yet, or are you stuck here like me?
chatA: (laughing) Don't even get me started, I got two hours of stories.
Girl, no. Tell me she did not say that.
Take yours, I'm good, I ate a big lunch.
Ugh, mine was off by four bucks, I stayed late counting.
New guy? Bless his heart, he's trying at least.
Nothing yet. You know they tell us last, like always.
(sighing) That fridge is a crime scene, I'm not touching it.
Not it. I did carts yesterday, my shirt's still sweaty.
Every week, man. I'm about done with this place.
Just pulled in, we're gonna be unloading for hours.
Yeah, grab me a large iced, I'll pay you back, promise.
(sighing) Third time this month. Must be nice.
That's you tonight, buddy, I closed all weekend.
Nope, still busted. I've been sweating since noon.
Friday? Great. Now I gotta actually face the shelves.
Returns all day. One guy brought back a half eaten cake.
Check the returns cart, that's where mine always ends up.
That was me, sorry, I'll make a fresh pot.
It's always the third register. Always. I'll grab the tools.
Two more hours. Somebody save me.

ask: Excuse me, where do you keep the milk?
Hi, is this one gluten free?
How much is this? There's no price on it.
Do you guys carry oat milk?
Which aisle has the pasta sauce?
Is this on sale, or did I read the sign wrong?
Where's your bathroom, if you don't mind?
You got any more of these in the back?
Excuse me, is the deli still open?
Do you take coupons here?
Where would I find the batteries?
Is this the express lane? It says twelve items.
Can you check if you have this in a large?
What aisle is the cereal on again?
Do y'all sell stamps here?
Hey, is the coffee fresh, or is that from this morning?
Where's the bread at? I've walked this whole store.
Is there a restroom near the front?
Excuse me, can somebody do a price check?
You got paper towels? I don't see them anywhere.
checkout: Oh, I don't need a bag, thanks.
Debit, please. Is the chip reader working?
Sorry, I think I forgot the eggs, hold on.
Did the coupon go through? It's for two dollars off.
Can you break a hundred? Sorry, that's all I got.
Just these, thanks, quick trip today.
Ooh, throw a pack of gum on there too.
You didn't ring up the water, did you?
Keep the receipt, I don't need it.
Whew, that's more than I thought. Groceries, man.
Cash back, twenty, if that's alright.
Take your time, no rush, I'm in no hurry.
excuse: Oops, excuse me, just reaching past you.
Sorry, scuse me, I need to grab that.
Pardon me, coming through with the cart.
Whoops, my bad, didn't see you there.
Can I just squeeze by real quick?
Sorry, I think that's my cart you got.
Excuse me, is this line moving?
Oh, go ahead, you were here first.
kids: Mom, can we get these? Pleeease?
But you SAID I could pick a snack!
I want the cereal with the toy in it!
Can I get a slushie? Can I? Can I?
Daddy, they have the big candy bars!
(whining) I'm bored, are we done yet?
pricecheck: This rang up wrong, it's supposed to be two ninety nine.
The sign said buy one get one, did I get that deal?
Wait, that's not the sale price, can you check?
How much was that? I want to make sure it's right.

publix_greet: Welcome to Publix! Let me know if you need anything.
Hey there, welcome in. Shopping or just browsing?
Afternoon! Baskets are right by the door.
Welcome! The deli's got samples out today if you want one.
starbucks_greet: Welcome in! What can I get started for you?
Hey! Grab a seat, I'll be right with you.
Morning! You know what you want, or need a sec?
dunkin_greet: Welcome to Dunkin, what'll it be?
Hey there, hot or iced today?
Come on up, the donuts are fresh.
sushi_greet: Irasshaimase! Welcome! Bar seat for you?
Come in, come in, I make you something special.
bank_greet: Welcome to Regions. Next available window.
Afternoon. You here for an account, or just cashing a check?
Step on up, sir, what can we do for you.
dollar_greet: Welcome in! Everything's a dollar, well, mostly.
Hey hon, holler if you need help finding anything.
Come on in, we just restocked.
help: You look a little lost, can I help you find something?
Need a hand finding anything, hon?
found: Did you find everything you were looking for today?
loiter: You've been standing there a while, everything alright?
noloiter: (angry) Hey! No loitering. Buy something or move along.
